title: Cystic fibrosis = obstructive PFTs
vignette: 25-year-old with cystic fibrosis and recurrent pulmonary exacerbations. PFTs (TLC / FEV1:FVC / FVC / RV)?

clues.note: CFTR defect -> bronchiectasis -> obstructive lung disease.

whatMatters: CF is obstructive: low FEV1/FVC, air trapping (high RV, high TLC), low FVC.
strategy: Classify by FEV1/FVC ratio: down = obstructive (then check air trapping); normal/up = restrictive (small lungs).

options.text: TLC up / ratio up / FVC normal / RV up
TLC down / ratio up / FVC down / RV down
TLC up / ratio down / FVC down / RV down
TLC up / ratio down / FVC down / RV up
TLC up / ratio normal / FVC up / RV normal

eliminations.correctIf: PFTs showed normal or elevated FEV1/FVC ratio (non-obstructive or hyperventilation pattern)

eliminations.why: Everything small; opposite of CF. Trap.
eliminations.correctIf: Patient had pulmonary fibrosis, sarcoidosis, or a neuromuscular disease causing true restrictive pattern
Patient was acutely hyperventilating (washing out air, transiently low RV) — not obstructive air trapping
PFTs were entirely normal (early/mild CF without airflow obstruction yet)

reasoning: Air trapping -> high RV and TLC; FVC falls
TLC up, ratio down, FVC down, RV up -> D
mechanism: CF obstruction limits expiratory flow (low FEV1/FVC); trapped air raises RV and TLC (hyperinflation) and reduces FVC. Restrictive disease is the opposite (low TLC/RV, preserved ratio).

keywords.back: Obstructive: TLC up, FEV1/FVC down, FVC down, RV up
Low TLC, low RV, normal/high ratio

memoryHook: Can't get air out -> it gets trapped (RV and TLC go up).
